scholarships.title: Post-9/11 GI Bill (Chapter 33) — Transferred Benefit
scholarships.description: Covers 100% of in-state tuition & fees at any Virginia public university (ODU, VCU, JMU, UVA, Tech, etc.). Also includes a monthly housing stipend (BAH at E-5 w/ dependents) and $1,000/yr book allowance.

scholarships.title: Yellow Ribbon Program
scholarships.description: If you choose a private school or out-of-state university, the Yellow Ribbon Program can cover the gap above the in-state rate. The school and VA split the difference — can cover full tuition at participating schools.

scholarships.title: Apply for GI Bill Benefits — VA.gov
scholarships.description: Submit VA Form 22-1990e (Application for Transfer of Entitlement) to officially claim the transferred benefit. Do this early — processing can take several weeks before school starts.

scholarships.title: Virginia Academic Scholarship (VAS)
scholarships.description: Merit-based award for high-achieving Virginia public school graduates. Applied through your high school counselor — not a separate application. Up to $3,000/year stackable on top of GI Bill.

scholarships.title: Virginia Guaranteed Assistance Program (VGAP)
scholarships.description: Need-based grant for Virginia residents at participating public colleges. Apply via FAFSA — the school awards it automatically if eligible. Covers remaining costs after other aid.

scholarships.title: SCHEV — All Virginia Scholarships & Grants
scholarships.description: State Council of Higher Education for Virginia — the official hub for all VA state financial aid programs including teaching scholarships, STEM awards, and need-based grants.

scholarships.title: Survivors' & Dependents' Educational Assistance (DEA / Ch. 35)
scholarships.description: Alternative to transferred GI Bill — pays up to ~$1,471/month for full-time enrollment. Can sometimes be better depending on the school. Check both benefits before choosing.

scholarships.title: Military Child Education Coalition (MCEC) Scholarships
scholarships.description: Scholarships specifically for children of military members, including multiple awards for dependents of active duty, veterans, and National Guard. Hampton Roads students eligible.

scholarships.title: Pat Tillman Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Prestigious scholarship for military veterans and dependents — up to $25,000/yr. Highly competitive but well worth applying. Strong essay component.

scholarships.title: AMVETS National Scholarship
scholarships.description: Open to children and grandchildren of veterans, active duty, National Guard, and Reserve. Multiple scholarship tiers from $1,000–$4,000. Virginia residents eligible.

scholarships.title: Hampton Roads Community Foundation
scholarships.description: Local foundation offering dozens of scholarships for Hampton Roads students — many are need and merit based, with several specifically for military families.

scholarships.title: Ivy Foundation of Hampton, Inc.
scholarships.description: Scholarships for Hampton-area students. Check the website for current application cycles and eligibility requirements — deadlines change yearly.

scholarships.title: Navy-Marine Corps Relief Society Education Assistance
scholarships.description: Financial assistance and scholarships for dependents of Navy and Marine Corps members — highly relevant for Hampton Roads families near NAS Oceana and Norfolk Naval Station.

scholarships.title: Fastweb — Free Scholarship Matching
scholarships.description: Create a free profile and Fastweb matches you to scholarships you qualify for — including military, Virginia-specific, and academic awards. One of the largest scholarship databases.

scholarships.title: Scholarships.com — Military Dependent Search
scholarships.description: Filter scholarships by military affiliation, state (Virginia), and school year. Good for finding awards that stack on top of GI Bill benefits.

scholarships.title: FAFSA — Free Application for Federal Student Aid
scholarships.description: File FAFSA even if you have GI Bill benefits — it unlocks additional grants (Pell, VGAP) that can be stacked on top. File as early as possible each year (opens October 1).

scholarships.title: American Legion Auxiliary Children of Warriors National Presidents' Scholarship
scholarships.description: Awarded to children of veterans and active-duty military. Covers tuition, room and board, books, and fees.

scholarships.title: Dominion Energy Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Provides scholarships to high school seniors and current college students in Virginia, including the Hampton Roads area.

scholarships.title: Southern Regional Education Board's Tuition Grant Program
scholarships.description: Offers tuition assistance for Virginia residents attending eligible colleges and universities, including those in the Hampton Roads area.

scholarships.title: Virginia War Memorial Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Awarded to children and grandchildren of Virginia veterans. Covers tuition, fees, and other educational expenses.

scholarships.title: Newman's Own Foundation Veterans Leadership Program
scholarships.description: Provides scholarships and leadership training to veterans and military dependents, including those who may have GI Bill benefits.

scholarships.title: Folds of Honor Scholarship
scholarships.description: Provides educational scholarships to spouses and children of America's fallen and disabled service members. Covers tuition, books, and fees.

scholarships.title: Virginia Senators Military Family Relief Fund Scholarship
scholarships.description: Provides financial assistance to children of Virginia military members who were killed or disabled while on active duty. Covers tuition, room and board, fees, and books.

scholarships.title: TCSPP Military Dependent Scholarship
scholarships.description: Offers scholarships to dependent children of active duty, reserve, or retired U.S. military personnel attending The Chicago School of Professional Psychology.

scholarships.title: Thurgood Marshall College Fund Scholarship
scholarships.description: Provides scholarships to students attending publicly-supported Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in Virginia, including Hampton University and Norfolk State University.

scholarships.title: Hope For The Warriors Scholarship
scholarships.description: Offers scholarships to spouses, children, siblings, or direct descendants of service members who have been killed or disabled in combat operations or training since 9/11.

scholarships.title: American Legion Legacy Scholarship
scholarships.description: Provides college scholarships to children of U.S. military members who died while on active duty on or after September 11, 2001.

scholarships.title: Virginia War Memorial Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.description: Offers scholarships to Virginia high school seniors who are children or grandchildren of Virginia veterans who served in wars since World War II.

scholarships.title: Virginia Military Survivors and Dependents Education Program
scholarships.description: Provides tuition assistance for children and spouses of military service members killed, disabled, or missing in action to attend public universities and colleges in Virginia.

scholarships.title: Navy-Marine Corps Relief Society Education Assistance
scholarships.description: Offers interest-free loans and grants to help dependents of active-duty, retired, and deceased Navy and Marine Corps service members with educational expenses.

scholarships.title: Wounded Warrior Project Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Provides scholarships to children and spouses of wounded, ill, or injured service members and veterans to help cover the cost of higher education.
